name: QRadar-0008 - DNS Tunneling and Covert Channel Detection
description: Detects DNS-based covert channels used by attackers to exfiltrate data or establish C2 communications. Identifies hosts generating abnormally high volumes of DNS requests to a single destination.

detection.log_source_type: DNS Server Logs, Network Flows (QFlow), Firewall Logs
detection.aql: SELECT sourceIP, destinationIP, COUNT(*) as query_count FROM events WHERE (destinationPort = 53 OR sourcePort = 53) AND LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%dns%' GROUP BY sourceIP, destinationIP HAVING COUNT(*) >= 30 LAST 10 MINUTES

response.offense_description: An internal host is generating anomalous DNS traffic indicative of tunneling. Very high query volume suggests data exfiltration or C2 over DNS.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rules: net_susp_dns_txt_query. Simplified for QRadar compatibility — removed LENGTH() and SUBDOMAIN_COUNT(). Consider DNS RPZ for automated blocking.
author: Security Team
